[L1] # CLAUDE.md — CodeMarker v2
[L2] 
[L3] ## Project Overview
[L4] 
[L5] Obsidian plugin for qualitative text coding (QDA). Rewrite of `mqda` on CodeMirror 6.
[L6] - **Location:** `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-codemarker-v2/`
[L7] - **Build:** `npm run build` (tsc + esbuild)
[L8] - **Entry:** `src/main.ts`
[L9] - **Branch:** `feat/hover-menu`
[L10] - **Vision:** Evolve into a full QDA platform — see `ARCHITECTURE.md` (Partes 6-9)
[L11] 
[L12] ## Critical Rules
[L13] 
[L14] - **NEVER modify `cm6TooltipMenu.ts` (Approach B)** — it's stable and the user explicitly protects it
[L15] - **NEVER modify `obsidianMenu.ts` (Approach A)** — it's stable, kept as fallback
[L16] - **Active development is on `cm6NativeTooltipMenu.ts` (Approach C)**
[L17] - When making menu changes, always test all 3 approaches haven't regressed
[L18] - Build must pass (`npm run build`) before declaring anything done
[L19] 
[L20] ## Architecture Quick Reference
[L21] 
[L22] | Layer | Files |
[L23] |-------|-------|
[L24] | CM6 Engine | `src/cm6/markerStateField.ts`, `markerViewPlugin.ts`, `selectionMenuField.ts`, `handleWidget.ts` |
[L25] | CM6 Hover | `src/cm6/hoverMenuExtension.ts` |
[L26] | CM6 Visual | `src/cm6/marginPanelExtension.ts` (MAXQDA-style bars) |
[L27] | CM6 Utils | `src/cm6/utils/viewLookupUtils.ts`, `markerPositionUtils.ts` |
[L28] | Menu (active) | `src/menu/cm6NativeTooltipMenu.ts` (Approach C) |
[L29] | Menu (stable) | `src/menu/obsidianMenu.ts` (A), `cm6TooltipMenu.ts` (B) |
[L30] | Menu shared | `src/menu/menuController.ts`, `menuActions.ts`, `menuTypes.ts` |
[L31] | Modal | `src/menu/codeFormModal.ts` |
[L32] | Data | `src/models/codeMarkerModel.ts`, `codeDefinitionRegistry.ts`, `settings.ts` |
[L33] | Views | `src/views/codeExplorerView.ts` (Code Explorer tree), `codeDetailView.ts` (list + code detail + marker detail), `settingsTab.ts` |
[L34] | UI | `styles.css` |
[L35] 
[L36] ## Known Patterns
[L37] 
[L38] ### CM6 Tooltip + Dark Mode
[L39] CSS variables from Obsidian **do not cascade** into CM6 tooltip DOM. The solution is `applyThemeColors()` in `cm6NativeTooltipMenu.ts` — reads computed values from `document.body` and copies them as inline styles + CSS custom properties onto the container.
[L40] 
[L41] ### Selection Preview
[L42] CM6 drops visual selection when focus moves to tooltip inputs or modals. Workaround: `setSelectionPreviewEffect` creates a `Decoration.mark` that visually mimics the selection. Must be dispatched **before** the tooltip/modal opens. Used in:
[L43] - `menuController.ts` — before tooltip opens
[L44] - `main.ts` — before CodeFormModal opens (command trigger)
[L45] - `cm6NativeTooltipMenu.ts` — before CodeFormModal opens (tooltip "Add New Code" button)
[L46] 
[L47] ### Menu Recreation
[L48] When user types a new code name and presses Enter, the tooltip closes and reopens with the new toggle. This is done via `onRecreate()` which batches `showCodingMenuEffect.of(null)` + `setSelectionPreviewEffect.of(null)`, then re-dispatches both after a 50ms timeout.

Title: DEVELOPMENT.md
Citation Marker: fileciteturn1file2
Mclick Target: "1:2"
Content Snippet:
[L1] ---
[L2] 
[L3] ## Feb 2026 — Hover Menu (v0)
[L4] 
[L5] ### Problem
[L6] 
[L7] No way to view or edit codes on existing markers. Users had to remember what codes were applied.
[L8] 
[L9] ### Solution
[L10] 
[L11] `hoverMenuExtension.ts` — a ViewPlugin that detects mouse hover over marker decorations and opens the same Approach C tooltip menu.
[L12] 
[L13] **Key design decisions:**
[L14] - Reuse existing menu (not a separate tooltip) — UX consistency
[L15] - 350ms open delay (avoid accidental activation), 200ms close delay (avoid flickering)
[L16] - `hoverMarkerId` field on `SelectionSnapshot` distinguishes hover mode from selection mode
[L17] - Custom events bridge tooltip DOM ↔ extension for mouse tracking
[L18] - Selection menu takes priority over hover menu when text is selected
[L19] 
[L20] **Known v0 bugs:** Handle drag while hover menu is open leaves menu visible + selection preview lingering.
[L21] 
[L22] ---
[L23] 
[L24] ## Feb 2026 — CodeDefinition Registry (Fase 1)
[L25] 
[L26] ### Problem
[L27] 
[L28] Codes were bare strings with no persistent identity. Colors lived on markers, not codes. No way to list all codes, assign consistent colors, or build a Code Explorer.
[L29] 
[L30] ### Solution
[L31] 
[L32] `codeDefinitionRegistry.ts` — a registry that gives each code a persistent identity:
[L33] - 12-color auto-palette with categorical distinctiveness
[L34] - `create()`, `update()`, `delete()`, `getByName()`, `getAll()`
[L35] - Auto-migration: on first load, extracts codes from existing markers into the registry
[L36] - `consumeNextPaletteColor()` assigns colors sequentially
[L37] 
[L38] **Impact:** Foundation for all downstream features (Code Explorer, Leaf View, per-code decorations).
[L39] 
[L40] ---
[L41] 
[L42] ## Feb 2026 — Margin Panel (MAXQDA-style)
[L43] 
[L44] ### Problem
[L45] 
[L46] No visual overview of which codes are applied where. Users had to hover each marker individually.
[L47] 
[L48] ### Solution
[L49] 
[L50] `marginPanelExtension.ts` (539 LOC) — renders colored bars in the left margin, one per code per marker:
[L51] 
[L52] **Layout algorithm:**
[L53] 1. **Column allocation by span:** Larger bars (covering more text) get rightmost columns (closest to text). This prevents wide bars from overlapping narrow ones.
[L54] 2. **Label positioning:** Labels centered on bars with weighted collision avoidance. Larger bars have higher weight, so they keep their label centered while smaller bars shift.
[L55] 3. **Dynamic sizing:** Measures actual text width for label space allocation.
[L56] 
[L57] **Technical details:**
[L58] - Detects natural left margin from RLL (Readable Line Length) plugin
[L59] - ResizeObserver for viewport changes + MutationObserver for DOM mutations
[L60] - Bidirectional hover via `setHoverEffect` (panel <-> editor text)
[L61] - `detectElementType()` differentiates bar/label/dot/tick for underline behavior
[L62] - `applyHoverClasses()` at end of `renderBrackets()` to survive DOM rebuilds
[L63] - `requestAnimationFrame` for smooth scroll tracking
[L64] 
[L65] ---
[L66] 
[L67] ## Feb 2026 — Margin Panel Bidirectional Hover
[L68] 
[L69] ### Problem
[L70] 
[L71] Margin panel labels had no interactive hover feedback. Underline CSS existed but never appeared due to a race condition: `applyHoverClasses()` would add the class, then `setHoverEffect` dispatch would trigger decoration rebuild, MutationObserver would fire, `renderBrackets()` would wipe DOM via `innerHTML = ''`, and hover classes were lost.
[L72] 
[L73] ### Solution
[L74] 
[L75] 1. **Race condition fix:** Call `applyHoverClasses()` at the end of `renderBrackets()` so hover state survives DOM rebuilds.
[L76] 
[L77] 2. **Element type detection:** `detectElementType()` identifies bar/label/dot/tick, enabling differentiated hover:
[L78]    - Bar/dot/tick hover → all labels of that marker get underline
[L79]    - Label hover → only that specific label gets underline

# 【6†DEVELOPMENT.md†file_000000001b24720ea888d6f39493fdc2】
Content source: Source.file
Title: DEVELOPMENT.md
Citation Marker: fileciteturn1file6
Mclick Target: "1:6"
Content Snippet:
[L1] # CodeMarker v2 — Development Journey
[L2] 
[L3] ## Origin
[L4] 
[L5] CodeMarker v2 is a rewrite of the `mqda` plugin (qualitative text coding for Obsidian). The original used Obsidian's legacy editor API with HTML spans. This version was rebuilt from scratch on CodeMirror 6 (CM6) decorations, state fields, and view plugins.
[L6] 
[L7] The core engine (markers, highlights, handles, drag-resize, persistence) was implemented successfully. The **floating menu** became the central challenge — and the problem that had blocked progress in the past.
[L8] 
[L9] ---
[L10] 
[L11] ## The Menu Problem
[L12] 
[L13] The mqda original had a custom floating menu built with Obsidian's native `Menu` class, injecting `TextComponent` and `ToggleComponent` into menu items via `(item as any).dom`. This gave native look-and-feel with theme support.
[L14] 
[L15] The challenge in v2: CM6 manages its own selection state. When an Obsidian `Menu` opens, it steals focus from the editor, and CM6 drops the selection highlight. The user can't see what text they're coding.
[L16] 
[L17] ### Three approaches were tried:
[L18] 
[L19] ---
[L20] 
[L21] ### Approach A — Obsidian Native Menu + Selection Preview
[L22] 
[L23] **File:** `src/menu/obsidianMenu.ts`
[L24] 
[L25] Uses Obsidian's `Menu` API with `TextComponent` and `ToggleComponent` injected into menu items (same pattern as mqda). Before opening, dispatches a `setSelectionPreviewEffect` that creates a `Decoration.mark` mimicking the native selection highlight.
[L26] 
[L27] **Result:** Selection stays visible via the decoration workaround. But the menu renders as Obsidian's standard context menu — functional but visually plain. The native components (TextComponent, ToggleComponent) are there, but the menu frame is Obsidian's default.
[L28] 
[L29] **Status:** Working. Kept as fallback option.
[L30] 
[L31] ---
[L32] 
[L33] ### Approach B — CM6 Tooltip (HTML)
[L34] 
[L35] **File:** `src/menu/cm6TooltipMenu.ts`
[L36] 
[L37] Builds a menu entirely from HTML elements (`<input>`, SVG icons, `<div>` items) rendered inside a CM6 `Tooltip`. Since the tooltip lives within CM6's DOM tree, the editor never loses focus, so the selection should stay active natively.
[L38] 
[L39] **Result:** Clean HTML menu, but selection highlight disappears when the input inside the tooltip receives focus. Also incomplete — was missing 3 of the 4 action buttons. Uses SVG circle icons instead of native toggle switches.
[L40] 
[L41] **Status:** Working but incomplete. Kept as-is for potential future use.
[L42] 
[L43] ---
[L44] 
[L45] ### Approach C — CM6 Tooltip + Native Obsidian Components (Winner)
[L46] 
[L47] **File:** `src/menu/cm6NativeTooltipMenu.ts`
[L48] 
[L49] Combines both approaches:
[L50] - **CM6 Tooltip** for the container (positioned at selection, inside editor DOM)
[L51] - **Obsidian TextComponent & ToggleComponent** for native look-and-feel
[L52] - **Selection Preview decoration** (same workaround as Approach A)
[L53] - **Theme color injection** — reads computed CSS variables from `document.body` and applies them as inline styles + custom properties on the tooltip container
[L54] 
[L55] **Result:** Full mqda design with native components, theme-aware (dark/light), selection stays highlighted, all action buttons present.
[L56] 
[L57] **Status:** Active. This is the recommended approach.
[L58] 
[L59] ---
[L60] 
[L61] ## The Dark Mode Breakthrough
[L62] 
[L63] The critical discovery was that **CSS variables don't cascade into CM6 tooltips**. The tooltip DOM lives inside `.cm-tooltip` which is part of CM6's rendering tree, disconnected from Obsidian's theme variable scope.
[L64] 
[L65] **Failed attempts:**
[L66] 1. `color-scheme: inherit` on the container — no effect
[L67] 2. `.theme-dark` / `.theme-light` CSS selectors targeting the tooltip — variables still not available
[L68] 3. `!important` on CSS variable references — variables resolve to empty

[L23] **File:** `src/menu/obsidianMenu.ts`
[L24] 
[L25] Uses Obsidian's `Menu` API with `TextComponent` and `ToggleComponent` injected into menu items (same pattern as mqda). Before opening, dispatches a `setSelectionPreviewEffect` that creates a `Decoration.mark` mimicking the native selection highlight.
[L26] 
[L27] **Result:** Selection stays visible via the decoration workaround. But the menu renders as Obsidian's standard context menu — functional but visually plain. The native components (TextComponent, ToggleComponent) are there, but the menu frame is Obsidian's default.
[L28] 
[L29] **Status:** Working. Kept as fallback option.
[L30] 
[L31] ---
[L32] 
[L33] ### Approach B — CM6 Tooltip (HTML)
[L34] 
[L35] **File:** `src/menu/cm6TooltipMenu.ts`
[L36] 
[L37] Builds a menu entirely from HTML elements (`<input>`, SVG icons, `<div>` items) rendered inside a CM6 `Tooltip`. Since the tooltip lives within CM6's DOM tree, the editor never loses focus, so the selection should stay active natively.
[L38] 
[L39] **Result:** Clean HTML menu, but selection highlight disappears when the input inside the tooltip receives focus. Also incomplete — was missing 3 of the 4 action buttons. Uses SVG circle icons instead of native toggle switches.
[L40] 
[L41] **Status:** Working but incomplete. Kept as-is for potential future use.
[L42] 
[L43] ---
[L44] 
[L45] ### Approach C — CM6 Tooltip + Native Obsidian Components (Winner)
[L46] 
[L47] **File:** `src/menu/cm6NativeTooltipMenu.ts`
[L48] 
[L49] Combines both approaches:
[L50] - **CM6 Tooltip** for the container (positioned at selection, inside editor DOM)
[L51] - **Obsidian TextComponent & ToggleComponent** for native look-and-feel
[L52] - **Selection Preview decoration** (same workaround as Approach A)
[L53] - **Theme color injection** — reads computed CSS variables from `document.body` and applies them as inline styles + custom properties on the tooltip container
[L54] 
[L55] **Result:** Full mqda design with native components, theme-aware (dark/light), selection stays highlighted, all action buttons present.
[L56] 
[L57] **Status:** Active. This is the recommended approach.
[L58] 
[L59] ---
[L60] 
[L61] ## The Dark Mode Breakthrough
[L62] 
[L63] The critical discovery was that **CSS variables don't cascade into CM6 tooltips**. The tooltip DOM lives inside `.cm-tooltip` which is part of CM6's rendering tree, disconnected from Obsidian's theme variable scope.
[L64] 
[L65] **Failed attempts:**
[L66] 1. `color-scheme: inherit` on the container — no effect
[L67] 2. `.theme-dark` / `.theme-light` CSS selectors targeting the tooltip — variables still not available
[L68] 3. `!important` on CSS variable references — variables resolve to empty

Title: DEVELOPMENT.md
Citation Marker: fileciteturn1file9
Mclick Target: "1:9"
Content Snippet:
[L1] ### Settings: `menuMode`
[L2] 
[L3] | Value | Approach | Description |
[L4] |-------|----------|-------------|
[L5] | `obsidian-native` | A | Obsidian Menu + preview decoration |
[L6] | `cm6-tooltip` | B | CM6 Tooltip with HTML elements |
[L7] | `cm6-native-tooltip` | C | CM6 Tooltip + native Obsidian components |
[L8] 
[L9] ---
[L10] 
[L11] ## Key Lessons
[L12] 
[L13] 1. **CM6 tooltips are isolated from Obsidian's CSS scope.** Don't rely on CSS variable inheritance. Copy computed values explicitly.
[L14] 
[L15] 2. **Don't dispatch CM6 effects inside `Tooltip.create()`.** The `create()` callback runs during a view update — dispatching there causes recursive updates and breaks the editor. Dispatch before (in the controller) or after (via `requestAnimationFrame`).
[L16] 
[L17] 3. **Batch related effects in a single `dispatch()`.** Selection preview and tooltip open/close should happen in the same transaction to avoid intermediate states.
[L18] 
[L19] 4. **Obsidian's `TextComponent` and `ToggleComponent` are just DOM elements.** They can be created inside any container — CM6 tooltip, modal, sidebar, whatever. The `(item as any).dom` pattern from mqda works everywhere.
[L20] 
[L21] 5. **Selection preview is needed even inside CM6 tooltips** if any child element (like TextComponent's input) receives focus. CM6 drops the visual selection when focus moves to an input, even if it's inside the editor DOM.
[L22] 
[L23] 6. **MutationObserver for layout shifts.** Some DOM changes (inline title toggle, theme switches) don't trigger CM6's resize/viewport events. `handleWidget.ts` and `marginPanelExtension.ts` use MutationObserver on `.cm-editor` to catch these.
[L24] 
[L25] 7. **Deferred deletion keeps hover menus coherent.** When toggling all codes off, `keepIfEmpty` flag prevents immediate marker deletion. The menu stays functional. Cleanup happens on close.
[L26] 
[L27] ---
[L28] 
[L29] ## Feb 2026 — Hover Menu (v0)
[L30] 
[L31] ### Problem
[L32] 
[L33] No way to view or edit codes on existing markers. Users had to remember what codes were applied.
[L34] 
[L35] ### Solution
[L36] 
[L37] `hoverMenuExtension.ts` — a ViewPlugin that detects mouse hover over marker decorations and opens the same Approach C tooltip menu.
[L38] 
[L39] **Key design decisions:**
[L40] - Reuse existing menu (not a separate tooltip) — UX consistency
[L41] - 350ms open delay (avoid accidental activation), 200ms close delay (avoid flickering)
[L42] - `hoverMarkerId` field on `SelectionSnapshot` distinguishes hover mode from selection mode
[L43] - Custom events bridge tooltip DOM ↔ extension for mouse tracking
[L44] - Selection menu takes priority over hover menu when text is selected
[L45] 
[L46] **Known v0 bugs:** Handle drag while hover menu is open leaves menu visible + selection preview lingering.
[L47] 
[L48] ---
[L49] 
[L50] ## Feb 2026 — CodeDefinition Registry (Fase 1)
[L51] 
[L52] ### Problem
[L53] 
[L54] Codes were bare strings with no persistent identity. Colors lived on markers, not codes. No way to list all codes, assign consistent colors, or build a Code Explorer.
[L55] 
[L56] ### Solution
[L57] 
[L58] `codeDefinitionRegistry.ts` — a registry that gives each code a persistent identity:
[L59] - 12-color auto-palette with categorical distinctiveness
[L60] - `create()`, `update()`, `delete()`, `getByName()`, `getAll()`
[L61] - Auto-migration: on first load, extracts codes from existing markers into the registry
[L62] - `consumeNextPaletteColor()` assigns colors sequentially
[L63] 
[L64] **Impact:** Foundation for all downstream features (Code Explorer, Leaf View, per-code decorations).
[L65] 
[L66] ---
[L67] 
[L68] ## Feb 2026 — Margin Panel (MAXQDA-style)
[L69] 
[L70] ### Problem
[L71] 
[L72] No visual overview of which codes are applied where. Users had to hover each marker individually.
[L73]

[L1] interface QDAProject {
[L2]     id: string;
[L3]     name: string;
[L4]     created: string;
[L5]     codebook: { codes: Code[]; codeGroups: CodeGroup[]; };
[L6]     segments: Segment[];
[L7]     memos: Memo[];

[L9]     savedQueries: SavedQuery[];
[L10] }
[L11] ```
[L12] 
[L13] ---
[L14] 
[L15] ## Parte 8: Leaf View — Interface de Análise
[L16] 
[L17] ### Por que leaf view
[L18] 
[L19] O menu flutuante CM6 resolve a *entrada* de dados (marcar + atribuir código). Mas toda a camada analítica precisa de espaço próprio — como um software de verdade. Não tem como enfiar codebook hierárquico, text retrieval, matrizes e memos num tooltip.
[L20] 
[L21] ### Layout proposto
[L22] 
[L23] ```
[L24] ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
[L25] │  [Global ▾]  CodeMarker                              [⚙] [📤]  │
[L26] ├──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
[L27] │                  │                                              │
[L28] │  Codebook        │  [Segments] [Matrix] [Documents]            │
[L29] │  ──────────      │                                              │
[L30] │  🔍 Filter.    │  Segments for "Motivação" (5)               │
[L31] │                  │  ─────────────────────────────               │
[L32] │  ▼ Motivação (5) │  "trecho importante sobre."  Plan.md:12   │
[L33] │  ▼ Barreira  (3) │  "outro trecho relevante."   Notes.md:45  │
[L34] │  ▶ Percepção (1) │  "mais um exemplo de."       Draft.md:8   │
[L35] │  ▶ Satisfação(2) │                                              │
[L36] │                  │                                              │
[L37] │  ──────────      │                                              │
[L38] │  + New Code      │                                              │
[L39] │                  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
[L40] │                  │  Memo: Este código captura as motivações     │
[L41] │                  │  intrínsecas dos participantes.            │
[L42] │                  │                                              │
[L43] ├──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
[L44] │  6 codes · 11 segments · Global Workspace                       │
[L45] └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
[L46] ```
[L47] 
[L48] **Áreas:**
[L49] - **Sidebar esquerda:** Codebook em árvore. Seletor Global/Projeto no topo. Quando no global, mostra todos os códigos. Quando num projeto, indica quais vieram do global.
[L50] - **Área central:** Tabs — text retrieval (segmentos por código), matriz de coocorrência, visão de documentos com marcações.
[L51] - **Painel inferior:** Memos, propriedades do código selecionado, estatísticas rápidas.
[L52] 
[L53] ### Implementação
[L54] 
[L55] - API: `ItemView` → `registerView()` → `workspace.getLeaf()` (pode ser tab central ou sidebar)
[L56] - Reatividade: event system pub/sub — plugin emite ao mudar markers, view escuta e atualiza
[L57] - DOM: manual ou framework leve. Obsidian não oferece reactive components nativamente.
[L58] 
[L59] ---
[L60] 
[L61] ## Parte 9: Roadmap Consolidado
[L62] 
[L63] ### Grafo de dependências atualizado
[L64] 
[L65] ```
[L66]                     ┌─────────────────┐
[L67]                     │ CodeDefinition  │

Content Snippet:
[L1] ### Menu Action Buttons (Approach C)
[L2] 3 action buttons in the tooltip footer:
[L3] 1. **Add New Code** (`plus-circle`) — opens CodeFormModal, reopens tooltip on close
[L4] 2. **Add Existing Code** (`tag`) — stub ("coming soon"), toggles already cover this
[L5] 3. **Remove Codes** (`trash`) — `removeAllCodesAction`, removes all codes from marker
[L6] 
[L7] ### Deferred Deletion (Hover Mode)
[L8] When user toggles all codes off during hover, the marker stays alive (`keepIfEmpty = true` in `removeCodeAction`). This prevents the menu from flickering. Empty markers are cleaned up when the menu closes via `cleanupEmptyMarker()`.
[L9] 
[L10] ### Smart Marker Layering
[L11] `findSmallestMarkerAtPos()` in `markerPositionUtils.ts` — resolves which marker to interact with when multiple overlap:
[L12] - **Nested:** smallest marker wins (more specific)
[L13] - **Partial intersection:** rightmost start position wins
[L14] 
[L15] ### MutationObserver for Layout Shifts
[L16] Used in `handleWidget.ts` and `marginPanelExtension.ts`. Observes `.cm-editor` DOM with `childList + subtree + attributeFilter` to detect changes (inline title toggle, theme switches) that don't trigger CM6's resize/viewport events.
[L17] 
[L18] ### Margin Panel Layout
[L19] `marginPanelExtension.ts` implements MAXQDA-style colored bars in the margin:
[L20] - Column allocation by span (larger bars -> rightmost/closest columns)
[L21] - Labels centered on bars with weighted collision avoidance
[L22] - Detects natural left margin (RLL plugin) for positioning
[L23] - ResizeObserver + MutationObserver for scroll tracking
[L24] 
[L25] ### Margin Panel Hover (Bidirectional)
[L26] Hover interaction between margin panel and editor text, via `setHoverEffect`:
[L27] - **Text hover** -> panel highlights corresponding bars/labels (all codes of marker)
[L28] - **Bar/dot/tick hover** -> all labels of that marker get underline
[L29] - **Label hover** -> only that specific label gets underline
[L30] - **Mouse out** from any element -> underlines + handles clear (dispatch `setHoverEffect(null)`)
[L31] - `detectElementType()` differentiates bar/label/dot/tick for underline logic
[L32] - `applyHoverClasses()` called at end of `renderBrackets()` to survive DOM rebuilds (MutationObserver race condition fix)
[L33] 
[L34] ### Margin Panel Click → Code Detail Side Panel
[L35] Label click in margin panel opens/updates `CodeDetailView` (ItemView) in the right sidebar:
[L36] - `panelClickHandler` reads `data-marker-id` + `data-code-name` from click target
[L37] - Falls back to hover state (`hoveredMarkerId`/`hoveredCodeName`) if DOM was rebuilt mid-click
[L38] - Calls `model.plugin.revealCodeDetailPanel(markerId, codeName)` → creates or updates sidebar leaf
[L39] - `revealLeaf()` only called on **first creation** — subsequent updates use `setContext()` only (avoids focus stealing)
[L40] - Click handler sets `suppressMutationUntil` before calling reveal to prevent MutationObserver render loop
[L41] 
[L42] ### MutationObserver Self-Trigger Prevention
[L43] `renderBrackets()` sets `suppressMutationUntil = Date.now() + 50` at the start. This prevents the panel's own DOM changes (innerHTML clear + element creation) from re-triggering the MutationObserver → scheduleUpdate → renderBrackets infinite loop. Without this, any action that triggers renderBrackets after `suppressMutationUntil` expires (e.g., focus change from `revealLeaf`) starts a render loop that destroys click targets.

[L1] ### Code Explorer — Tree View (`codeExplorerView.ts`)
[L2] `ItemView` in right sidebar — tree of all codes grouped by code → file → segments:
[L3] - **Level 1 (codeNodes):** code name + swatch + marker count
[L4] - **Level 2 (fileNodes):** file name + segment count per file
[L5] - **Level 3:** text preview of each segment, clickable → scrolls editor to marker
[L6] - **Toolbar:** All button (expand/collapse codeNodes only), Files button (expand/collapse fileNodes, auto-expands codes if collapsed), Refresh
[L7] - **Independent collapse levels:** `expandAll()`/`collapseAll()` only touch codeNodes; `expandFiles()`/`collapseFiles()` only touch fileNodes. Files button handler auto-expands codes when needed for visibility.
[L8] 
[L9] ### Code Detail View — Integrated Navigation (`codeDetailView.ts`)
[L10] `ItemView` in right sidebar with 3 navigation modes:
[L11] - **List mode** (`showList()`) — flat list of all codes with swatch, description, and segment count. Default when opened via command.
[L12] - **Code-focused detail** (`showCodeDetail(codeName)`) — shows all markers for a specific code across all files, with file reference and text preview. Reached by clicking a code in the list.
[L13] - **Marker-focused detail** (`setContext(markerId, codeName)`) — shows specific marker text, other codes (chips), other markers. Reached via margin panel label click.
[L14] - Both detail modes have a **back button** ("← All Codes") to return to the list.
[L15] - `revealCodeExplorer()` in main.ts opens this view in list mode.
[L16] - `revealCodeDetailPanel()` opens in marker-focused mode (margin panel integration).
[L17] - `getAllMarkers()` on model used for cross-file segment counting.
[L18] 
[L19] ## Settings
[L20] 
[L21] `menuMode` in settings controls which approach is active:
[L22] - `obsidian-native` -> Approach A
[L23] - `cm6-tooltip` -> Approach B
[L24] - `cm6-native-tooltip` -> Approach C (recommended)
